hints.text: Developed **city-state** — clean & orderly; divided main roads w/ **B&W kerbs + double yellow outer lines** + tall apartment blocks (often all you need)
Street signs: **green, rounded edges, distinct white font** — unique format; directional signs green/white English (expressways = 3-letter codes like TPE)
Plates **black with white text** (occasionally British white/yellow, rarely red)

shared_with.note: Black plates too — but usually split into **2 sections**.

hints.text: Road signs always on a **square white background**
**Rain trees** planted along main roads everywhere
Warnings written on the road in big letters (**HUMP AHEAD**)
Buses mostly **lime-green** (also white-purple SBS Transit); bus stops guarded by concrete bollards w/ **black-yellow warning bands**; yellow **bus-leaving box** on the street

shared_with.note: Similar (not identical) bus-stop bollards in some Malaysian cities.
shared_with.diff: Lime-green buses + orderly city-state look → Singapore.

hints.text: Traffic lights on a **curved, hole-punched metal sheet** support
Streets named after their **neighbourhood** (esp. suburbs) — e.g. 'Ang Mo Kio Street 31' → scan the map for the neighbourhood name
**Tuas** = industrial: straight roads, cranes, shipping containers; **Sentosa** = resort feel, winding roads, green signposts, sometimes a green lane
**Island trekkers**: Pulau Ubin = unusually rural forest + camo hat visible looking down; St John & Lazarus = smooth asphalt + sea nearby; **Kusu Island** = red paving stones on the whole path
